title: Centre de renseignement États-Unis — Mise à jour du 12 avr. 2026
summary: Les pourparlers de paix entre les États-Unis et l'Iran à Islamabad se sont effondrés après 21 heures le 12 avril, le VP Vance rentrant les mains vides — l'Iran a rejeté les exigences américaines d'engagement de non-prolifération nucléaire tandis que les États-Unis ont rejeté les demandes iraniennes de droits de péage sur Ormuz et de réparations de guerre. Trump a immédiatement ordonné à la marine américaine de bloquer le détroit d'Ormuz, provoquant une hausse du brut WTI de près de 6 % à ~105 $/baril et mettant sérieusement en péril le fragile cessez-le-feu de deux semaines. Les pourparlers — le premier engagement direct entre les États-Unis et l'Iran depuis 2015 — étaient menés par Vance aux côtés de l'envoyé spécial Steve Witkoff et de Jared Kushner. Par ailleurs, l'amerrissage réussi d'Artemis II le 10 avril au large de San Diego — un « atterrissage en plein dans le mille » selon le contrôle de mission — a été confirmé, les quatre astronautes ayant été récupérés sains et saufs par l'USS John P. Murtha.

title: United States Intelligence Hub Update — Sat Apr 11, 2026
summary: Vice President JD Vance arrived in Islamabad on April 11, 2026, leading the highest-level direct US-Iran engagement since the 2015 Obama-era nuclear talks.

title: Breaking: Kamala Harris Publicly Teases 2028 Presidential Bid
summary: Former Vice President Kamala Harris publicly signaled a potential return to presidential politics, teasing a 2028 presidential bid following Donald Trump's electoral victory over her in 2024.

title: United States Intelligence Hub Update — Apr 09, 2026
summary: Artemis II entered its final full day in space on April 9 — Flight Day 9 — with the crew packing for Thursday's Pacific Ocean splashdown as the USS John P. Murtha steamed to the San Diego recovery zone; splashdown remains confirmed for April 10 at ~5:07 p.m. PDT. The Iran ceasefire brokered by Trump on April 8 showed immediate strain as Israel launched what it called its 'largest attack on Lebanon' since Operation Roaring Lion began, with the Lebanon exclusion carve-out contradicting Pakistan's ceasefire framing — even as US and Iranian delegations prepared to travel to Islamabad for formal talks on April 10. An April 9 analysis found Trump's 646 deregulatory actions are being eclipsed economically by his own tariff regime, with net business investment headwinds masking deregulation gains.

title: United States Intelligence Hub Update — Apr 08, 2026
summary: Trump brokered a two-week Iran ceasefire on April 8, reached before his 8 p.m. ET deadline, with Iran agreeing to reopen the Strait of Hormuz — the first significant US-Iran diplomatic de-escalation in decades. US equity markets responded with a sharp rally, the S&P 500 gaining ~2.76% on ceasefire relief and falling oil prices, partially reversing the prior week's tariff-driven selloff. Trump simultaneously imposed 50% secondary tariffs on any nation supplying Iran with weapons, extending his tariff doctrine into foreign policy enforcement. The Artemis II crew completed Flight Day 8 return procedures with splashdown now targeting April 10 off San Diego, one day earlier than previously forecast.

title: United States Intelligence Hub Update — Apr 07, 2026
summary: US equity markets continued a sharp tariff-driven selloff on April 7, with the S&P 500 off roughly 10% from recent highs and JPMorgan CEO Dimon warning of rising recession probability — days after Trump shared a video framing the market decline as intentional. The Artemis II crew exited the lunar sphere of influence heading home after a record-breaking flyby, setting a new crewed distance mark at 252,760 miles from Earth and targeting Pacific splashdown around April 11. An ABC News review found Trump's legal team citing the 2024 SCOTUS presidential immunity ruling in nearly one-third of second-term emergency SCOTUS filings, spanning federal firings, immigration, and trade authority. NBC News also reported that US Marshals waived training requirements to deputize Musk's private security as armed federal agents during DOGE's government access phase.

title: United States Intelligence Hub Update — Apr 06, 2026
summary: Artemis II completed a historic lunar flyby on April 6 — the first humans near the Moon since Apollo 17 in 1972 — as restructured 50% Section 232 tariffs on steel, aluminum, and copper took effect simultaneously. Trump also imposed up to 100% tariffs on patented pharmaceutical imports (signed April 2), signed an executive order on voter citizenship verification (March 31), and the administration surpassed 2.5 million total immigration departures with ICE detention at a record ~68,000. DOGE's one-year mark drew competing analyses: $160B in claimed savings versus $135B in estimated disruption costs.

title: United States Intelligence Hub Update — Sun Apr 5, 2026
summary: DOGE One-Year Assessment: $160B Claimed Savings vs. $135B Estimated Disruption Costs.

title: United States Intelligence Hub Update — Sat Apr 4, 2026
summary: Trump Shares Video Claiming He Is Deliberately Crashing the Market to Force Fed Rate Cuts. DHS published an enforcement update on April 4 highlighting ICE arrests of gang members, sex offenders, and stalkers as part of ongoing interior enforcement operations.

title: United States Intelligence Hub Update — Fri Apr 3, 2026
summary: Trump Administration Surpasses 605,000 Formal Deportations; 2.5M Total Departures.

title: United States Intelligence Hub Update — Thu Apr 2, 2026
summary: Trump signed a proclamation under Section 232 restructuring tariffs on steel, aluminum, copper, and derivative products. Trump Imposes Up to 100% Tariff on Patented Pharmaceutical Imports. DHS announced that ICE, working with the U.S.

title: United States Intelligence Hub Update — Wed Apr 1, 2026
summary: NASA's Artemis II lifted off from Kennedy Space Center at 22:35 UTC, carrying astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch (NASA), and Jeremy Hansen (CSA).
